xopods: This move was an interesting direction of play question for me. Maybe we'll pose it to the thread before I do the review.
oiseaux: yeah. not so much by my intention, although I do like seeing white transform their influence

C[oiseaux: it's this mistake i make in every single game and its not until after i make it that i realise how obviously poor it is. i feel like i'm going backwards.

C[xopods: Well, here you're playing even games with a stronger opponent, so it's normal that you're falling behind and need to make some do-or-die play to get back in it. I don't have a problem with any of your attempts to shake... if it wasn't a correspondence game, it's not inconceivable that I'd screw up
xopods: In both games I think your more egregious overplay was trying to dive into my moyo with an extra-large knight's move when I'm thick on both sides.
C[oiseaux: yeah. i've lost sight of a lot of fundamentals

C[xopods: That's kind of part of getting stronger, though. You can't always just look at the safest, most obvious moves. Being able to break away from basic instinct at the right times is part of being a strong player, but you go through growing pains to get there
oiseaux: yeah. i'm glad for our games. :)
xopods: It might be next week before I get around to reviewing this one, but I've got a conversation going about the early middlegame in the Ask Go Players thread
oiseaux: Don't feel obliged to review if you don't have time, I appreciate the contest
oiseaux: Yeah I need to catch up with that convo
